skill.name: claudeemailmarketing
skill.description: Plan, review, and improve Claude-assisted email marketing workflows with positioning, segmentation, lifecycle copy, QA checks, and approval gates.
skill.summary: Guides safer Claude-assisted email strategy, copy review, campaign planning, and lifecycle messaging.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: The four static weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary prose and YAML field names. The reviewed files contain guidance for email planning, review boundaries, and approval gates, with no evidence of malware, secret access, network behavior, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3 and agents/openai.yaml line 2. These lines contain descriptive marketing text and YAML metadata, not cryptographic code or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence in an actual weak cryptography risk is very low. The matched lines are natural language and metadata, with no crypto API or executable code present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged references/operating-checklist.md lines 19 and 27. These checklist lines discuss review notes and high-risk email operations, not cryptographic functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confidence in an actual weak cryptography risk is very low. The content is a plain-language operating checklist and contains no executable security-sensitive logic.

content.value_statement: Email campaigns often fail when strategy, segment intent, and review ownership are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured email plans with evidence checks and approval gates.
actual_capabilities: Creates positioning briefs for email campaigns.
Builds segment message matrices for different audience needs.
Reviews lifecycle email copy against proof, CTA, and funnel stage.
Plans Claude prompt packs for controlled marketing ideation.
Defines experiment plans with clear variables and success metrics.
Separates recommendations from live email system actions.
limitations: Does not send emails or modify production marketing systems.
Requires approved product facts, proof, and brand constraints.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or deliverability review.
Cannot verify ESP data unless the user provides accurate context.

use_cases.title: Launch Campaign Planning
use_cases.description: Create an email angle, segment logic, proof plan, CTA, and review checklist before a product launch.

use_cases.title: Lifecycle Copy Review
use_cases.description: Audit onboarding, activation, retention, or winback emails for clarity, proof, timing, and measurement.

use_cases.title: Claude Prompt System Design
use_cases.description: Design reusable Claude prompts that keep email ideation inside approved evidence and brand constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan an email campaign for [product] and [segment]. Include the belief change, offer angle, proof, CTA, and approval needs.

prompt_templates.title: Review Lifecycle Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this lifecycle email for [funnel stage]. Check segment fit, claims, proof, CTA strength, risk, and the measurement plan.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Segment Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a segment message matrix for [audiences]. Compare situation, intent, main objection, angle, proof, CTA, and test priority.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Claude Prompt Pack
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Claude prompt pack for email strategy. Include intake questions, evidence boundaries, critique prompts, variant prompts, and approval gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a launch email for a project management tool aimed at agency owners.
output_examples.output: A campaign brief with the segment tension, offer angle, proof points, CTA, send rationale, review owners, and success metric.

output_examples.input: Review this activation email before it enters the lifecycle sequence.
output_examples.output: A copy review covering segment fit, unsupported claims, CTA alignment, objection handling, experiment risk, and required approvals.

output_examples.input: Create Claude prompts for weekly email testing.
output_examples.output: A prompt pack that asks Claude to ideate, critique, and compare variants while staying inside approved evidence and brand rules.

best_practices: Provide product facts, audience context, proof, and brand constraints before asking for copy.
Ask for the marketing decision and review owner, not only draft text.
Require explicit approval before live sends, imports, suppression changes, or automation changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the agent to invent proof, guarantees, customer quotes, or urgency.
Do not use one generic message for segments with different intent.
Do not treat recommendations as permission to change live email systems.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete email drafts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft and critique email copy when the user provides segment context, proof, and brand constraints.

faq.question: Does it send emails or change automations?
faq.answer: No. It separates recommendations from execution and requires explicit approval for high-risk operational actions.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide the offer, target segment, funnel stage, proof, brand voice, success metric, and review requirements.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define test variables, compare variants, and recommend metrics that isolate meaningful campaign changes.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows for email strategy and review.

faq.question: Can it replace legal or compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It can identify claims and approval needs, but human owners must review risky or regulated messaging.
